description: Fix Shift+Tab mode toggle so that cycling INTERACTIVE → PLAN → AUTOPILOT triggers a conversation-view notification, prefixes plan-mode messages with [[PLAN]] before sending to the AI, and injects the canonical plan-mode / autopilot system-prompt sections — matching the original Copilot CLI behaviour exactly.

userStories.title: Mode change notification via Textual message
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a system message in the conversation view when I press Shift+Tab so I can confirm which mode is now active.
userStories.notes: InputBar already has the backtab binding and action_cycle_mode(). This story just adds post_message and the MarchApp handler. post_message requires the widget to be mounted, so unit tests must mock it rather than calling it directly on an unmounted widget.
implementationNotes: In src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/input_bar.py, add a nested Textual Message class: `class ModeChanged(Message): def __init__(self, mode: InputMode) -> None: super().__init__(); self.mode = mode`. Place it inside the InputBar class body, before compose().
In InputBar.action_cycle_mode(), after updating self._mode and self._mode_label, add `self.post_message(InputBar.ModeChanged(self._mode))` as the last line of the method.

In src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py, add event handler `def on_input_bar_mode_changed(self, event: InputBar.ModeChanged) -> None:`. Inside it: query ConversationView with `conv = self.query_one(ConversationView)` and call `conv.add_message(MessageRole.SYSTEM, f'⚡ Mode changed to: {event.mode.value}')`. The InputBar import already exists via `from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets import ConversationView, InputBar`.
Add import `from textual.message import Message` in input_bar.py if not already present. Check existing imports first.
Do NOT change CYCLE_ORDER, MODE_COLORS, InputMode enum values, or existing BINDINGS. Do NOT change the backtab binding key string.
acceptanceCriteria: InputBar.ModeChanged is importable from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets.input_bar and is a subclass of textual.message.Message
InputBar.ModeChanged carries a .mode attribute of type InputMode
Unit test: calling action_cycle_mode() on an InputBar with a mocked post_message verifies post_message is called once with an InputBar.ModeChanged whose .mode equals the expected next mode
Unit test: MarchApp.on_input_bar_mode_changed calls ConversationView.add_message with MessageRole.SYSTEM and a string containing the new mode name
All pre-existing tests in tests/test_tui_rewrite.py continue to pass
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures
userStories.title: Message prefix injection based on mode
userStories.description: As a user in PLAN mode, I want my messages sent to the AI with [[PLAN]] prepended so the AI enters plan-mode behaviour, while the conversation view shows my original text without that prefix.
userStories.notes: The conversation view and agent history must store the original (unprefixed) text. Only the transient messages list passed to the AI client gets the [[PLAN]] prefix. The _stream_ai_response signature gains a second parameter `mode`.
implementationNotes: In MarchApp.on_input_submitted (tui_app.py), after the empty-string guard and before the slash-command branch, read the current mode: `input_bar = self.query_one('#input-bar', InputBar); current_mode = input_bar.current_mode`. Pass current_mode to the streaming call.
Update the call to _stream_ai_response to pass mode: change `self._stream_ai_response(text)` to `self._stream_ai_response(text, current_mode)`. Keep all other on_input_submitted logic unchanged.

Update MarchApp._stream_ai_response signature from `(self, user_text: str)` to `(self, user_text: str, mode: InputMode = InputMode.INTERACTIVE)`. Add `from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets.input_bar import InputBar, InputMode` near the top of tui_app.py if not already imported at module level (it is already there via the tui_widgets import, but InputMode needs an explicit import).

Inside _stream_ai_response, after building `messages` from agent.get_conversation_context() or the fallback list: if mode == InputMode.PLAN, find the last dict in messages where dict['role'] == 'user' and replace its 'content' with f'[[PLAN]] {user_text}'. Use the original user_text parameter, not the stored history value, to keep the prefix clean.
Do NOT modify agent.add_user_message(text) calls — agent history must remain unprefixed. Do NOT modify ConversationView.add_message calls — the displayed text must remain unprefixed.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit test: when InputBar._mode is set to InputMode.PLAN and on_input_submitted fires with text='hello', the ConversationView receives a USER message with content 'hello' (no [[PLAN]] prefix)
Unit test: _stream_ai_response with mode=InputMode.PLAN builds a messages list where the last user message content is '[[PLAN]] hello'
Unit test: _stream_ai_response with mode=InputMode.INTERACTIVE builds a messages list where the last user message content is 'hello' (no prefix)
Unit test: submitting empty or whitespace-only text does NOT add any message to ConversationView regardless of mode
Unit test: Agent.get_conversation_context() after on_input_submitted stores the original text without [[PLAN]] prefix
All pre-existing tests in tests/test_tui_rewrite.py continue to pass
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: System prompt injection for plan and autopilot modes
userStories.description: As a user, I want the AI to receive the correct mode-specific system prompt section so it behaves in full plan mode or full autopilot mode, exactly as in the original Copilot CLI.
userStories.notes: The plan-mode prompt text is copied verbatim from the original JS source (21-react-scheduler.js line 639-656). The autopilot prompt is copied verbatim from line 591. Avoid circular imports: plan_mode.py must import InputMode inside the function body only, guarded by TYPE_CHECKING for the type hint.
implementationNotes: Extend src/mARCH/core/plan_mode.py with two module-level string constants. PLAN_MODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT must be the exact text block (see PRD section US-003). AUTOPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT must be: 'You are running in non-interactive mode and have no way to communicate with the user. You must work on the task until it is completed. Do not stop to ask questions or request confirmation - make reasonable assumptions and proceed autonomously. Complete the entire task before finishing.' — these strings match the original JS source exactly.

Add `build_mode_system_prompt(base_prompt: str, mode: Any) -> str` to plan_mode.py. Inside the function body (not at module level), import: `from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets.input_bar import InputMode`. Compare mode with InputMode.PLAN → return base_prompt + '

' + PLAN_MODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT.strip(); InputMode.AUTOPILOT → return AUTOPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT.strip() + '

' + base_prompt; otherwise return base_prompt unchanged. Add `from __future__ import annotations` at top of plan_mode.py and use `from typing import TYPE_CHECKING` guard for the type annotation: `if TYPE_CHECKING: from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets.input_bar import InputMode`.

In MarchApp._stream_ai_response (tui_app.py), after building `messages`, augment the system message: find the first dict with role == 'system'. If found, replace its 'content' with build_mode_system_prompt(original_content, mode). If no system message exists in the list, prepend {'role': 'system', 'content': build_mode_system_prompt('', mode)}. Import build_mode_system_prompt at top of tui_app.py: `from mARCH.core.plan_mode import build_mode_system_prompt`.
Do NOT modify PlanModeDetector, its PLAN_PREFIX constant, is_plan_request(), or extract_content(). These remain unchanged.
The function signature annotation for mode in build_mode_system_prompt should use `Any` at runtime (to avoid circular import) with the TYPE_CHECKING guard providing the real type for linters.
acceptanceCriteria: PLAN_MODE_SYSTEM_PROMPT is importable from mARCH.core.plan_mode and contains the substrings '[[PLAN]]', 'plan mode', and 'plan.md'
AUTOPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT is importable from mARCH.core.plan_mode and contains 'non-interactive mode' and 'autonomously'
build_mode_system_prompt(base, InputMode.PLAN) returns a string containing '[[PLAN]]' and 'plan mode' and 'plan.md'
build_mode_system_prompt(base, InputMode.AUTOPILOT) returns a string that starts with the autopilot paragraph (contains 'non-interactive mode' and 'autonomously') followed by base
build_mode_system_prompt(base, InputMode.INTERACTIVE) returns base unchanged
Unit test: _stream_ai_response in PLAN mode produces a system message containing '[[PLAN]]' and 'plan mode'
Unit test: _stream_ai_response in AUTOPILOT mode produces a system message containing 'non-interactive mode' and 'autonomously'
PlanModeDetector.is_plan_request and .extract_content still work identically to before
No circular import: `python -c 'from mARCH.core.plan_mode import build_mode_system_prompt'` exits with code 0
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures
